logs.title: MBR (1575 lines) and EBR (1307 lines) ingested — AMX-2024-0093 loaded
reasoning: MBR reference: MPR-AMX-500-v07 — 1575 lines parsed
EBR batch: AMX-2024-0091 — 1307 lines parsed
Product: Amoxicillin Trihydrate Capsules 500mg, 50,000 capsule batch
Manufacturing date 22-Apr-2024; packaging 23-Apr-2024; QA release 28-Apr-2024
Both documents structured and ready for field-by-field 10-category GMP review

logs.title: 72-point checklist: 70 pass, 2 fail — label reconciliation + data integrity
reasoning: Category 1-3 (Header, BOM, Equipment): All pass — product ID correct, all 4 materials released, 9/9 equipment current
Category 4-6 (Process, IPC, Environment): All pass — blend 100.26% LC RSD 0.50%, fill weights 629-631 mg, all EM within spec
Category 7-8 (Yield/Reconciliation, QC): Label reconciliation FAIL — 13 unaccounted labels recorded as PASS; all 16 FP QC tests pass
Category 9-10 (Signatures, Deviations): Data integrity FAIL — correction on p.87 missing mandatory reason; no deviations raised for either finding
Total: 70 pass / 2 fail — Items 52 (label reconciliation) and 63 (correction reason) both FAIL

logs.title: 2 MAJOR findings confirmed — Confidence: 84% — HOLD
reasoning: Finding 1 (MAJOR): Label reconciliation — 50,500 issued vs 50,487 accounted = 13 unaccounted labels; operator falsely recorded discrepancy = 0 and PASS
Finding type: label_reconciliation — violates zero-tolerance rule in MBR Sections 5.5.5, 7.5, 8.4 and 21 CFR 211.125 / EU GMP Annex 15
Finding 2 (MAJOR): QC correction on p.87 (FP-04 peak area) missing mandatory reason field — violates MBR Section 7.2 and 21 CFR Part 211 data integrity
QA reviewer S. Iyer accepted both items without verifying the arithmetic or the correction reason
Confidence: 100 - (2 × 8 major) = 84% — HOLD band (50-87%)

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: 13 labels unaccounted (50,500 issued vs 50,487 accounted) — zero-tolerance breach per 21 CFR 211.125
Operator recorded discrepancy as 0 and marked PASS — incorrect data entry
QC correction on p.87 missing mandatory reason per MBR Section 7.2 — ALCOA+ violation
QA reviewer accepted both findings without independent verification
Expedited QA review required before any disposition decision

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — Label Reconciliation + Data Integrity
artifacts.subject: HOLD: 2 Major Findings — Batch AMX-2024-0093 Amoxicillin 500mg
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch AMX-2024-0093 (Amoxicillin Trihydrate Capsules 500mg) has been placed on HOLD.

MAJOR FINDING 1 — Label Reconciliation:
50,500 labels issued; 50,487 accounted for; discrepancy = 13 unaccounted labels. Operator recorded discrepancy as 0 (PASS) — arithmetically incorrect. Zero-tolerance breach per MBR Section 5.5.5, 7.5, 8.4, and 21 CFR 211.125.

MAJOR FINDING 2 — Data Integrity:
QC correction on EBR p.87 (FP-04 peak area, analyst T. Kumar) is missing the mandatory reason field required by MBR Section 7.2 and 21 CFR Part 211.

Required actions:
1. Verify label count arithmetic and locate 13 unaccounted labels
2. Obtain correction reason from T. Kumar before any release decision
3. Raise deviation report for both findings

Batch remains on HOLD pending resolution.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
